desc: Most abundant western NA bumble bee. Primary buzz pollinator of tomatoes and native wildflowers.

fm.Thorax: Black with bright yellow anterior band
fm.Abdomen: T1 yellow; T2–T3 black; T4 bright yellow; T5–T6 black
fm.Hair: Bright lemon-yellow on black; clean contrast
fm.Face: Yellow; short rounded; short tongue (¼ in–⅓ in)
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–¾ in; workers ⅓ in–⅔ in

desc: Large species in substantial decline since 1990s. Long tongue critical for deep-corolla wildflowers.

fm.Thorax: Yellow anterior; broad black between wings
fm.Abdomen: T1 yellow; T2–T3 yellow/black; T4 tawny
fm.Hair: Rich golden; some populations tawny T3–T4
fm.Face: Black facial hair; long tongue (⅓ in–½ in)
fm.Size: Queens ¾ in–1 in (among largest CA Bombus)

desc: CESA candidate endangered (2019 petition). Dramatic collapse — pesticides, habitat loss, pathogen spillover. Every iNat obscritical.

fm.Thorax: Yellow anterior; extensive black; thin yellow posterior
fm.Abdomen: T1 yellow; T2–T3 tawny-orange (diagnostic); T4 orange-brown
fm.Hair: Warm orange-brown unique in LA County Bombus
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–1 in; workers ½ in–⅔ in

desc: Robust desert-adapted species. Heat-tolerant; active in late afternoon.

fm.Thorax: Extensively yellow; narrow black between wings
fm.Abdomen: T1–T4 predominantly yellow; T5 mixed; T6 black
fm.Face: Yellow; broad rounded; medium tongue (⅓ in)
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–¾ in; workers ½ in–⅔ in

desc: Variable — orange and yellow morphs both in LA County. Higher elevations in San Gabriels and Santa Monicas.

fm.Thorax: Yellow or orange anterior band; black centrally
fm.Abdomen: T1–T2 orange or yellow; T3–T6 black (diagnostic "black tail")
fm.Hair: Orange morph: vivid T1–T2. Yellow morph: lemon bands.
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in; workers ⅓ in–½ in

desc: Southern range edge in LA County. Restricted to San Gabriel montane meadows above 4,000'.

fm.Thorax: Entirely bright yellow with minimal black
fm.Hair: Richest golden-yellow of any LA Bombus
fm.Face: Yellow; elongated; long tongue (⅓ in–½ in)
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–¾ in; workers ½ in–⅔ in

desc: Severe continent-wide decline. LA County records increasingly sparse.

fm.Thorax: Yellow anterior; broad black behind wings
fm.Abdomen: T1 yellow; T2 mixed; T3–T6 black
fm.Hair: Pale yellow with extensive black; muted
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–1 in; workers ½ in–⅔ in

desc: Distinctive pale bumble bee of arid habitats. Paler than any other LA Bombus.

fm.Abdomen: T1 pale; T2–T3 black; T4–T5 white/cream (pale tail)
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in–¾ in; workers ½ in

desc: Cryptic, confused with B. vosnesenskii. Darker, duller yellow, dark facial hair. Confirmed sightings valuable.

fm.Abdomen: T1 narrower yellow; T2–T3 black; T4 reduced; T5–T6 black
fm.Size: Queens ⅔ in; workers ⅓ in–½ in
